commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Brave's provider-local `mergeScopedSearchConfig` to define injected legacy web_search provider config as non-enumerable instead of using an enumerable object spread.

PR surface: Source +21. Total +21 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, but not as a live reproduction in this review: current main's Brave helper creates an enumerable legacy provider object, and the validator rejects enumerable legacy provider objects. The linked issue at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87191 also provides fresh-container startup failure context.
commands.review_followup: This useful PR needs maintainer review or contributor-supplied real gateway startup proof; there is no narrow code repair for automation to perform from the current review.

target.title: fix(web-search): apply legacy config fix to brave inline merge (#87191)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: PR #86818 fixed `mergeScopedSearchConfig` to hide runtime-injected legacy web_search config keys from validation via `Object.defineProperty` with `enumerable: false`. But the brave extension has its own inline copy of this function using the old spread pattern, which re-enumerates hidden legacy keys and causes gateway crash-loop on startup with "legacy web_search provider config must use plugins.entries.<plugin>.config.webSearch".
- Solution: Apply the same `Object.defineProperty` treatment from #86818 to the brave inline copy, so runtime-injected legacy config keys remain non-enumerable and pass validation.
- What changed: `extensions/brave/src/brave-web-search-provider.ts` — replaced the spread-based merge with `Object.defineProperty` that sets `enumerable: false` for legacy provider keys when the key has no existing own-property descriptor.
- What did NOT change: No other extensions were affected. The shared `mergeScopedSearchConfig` in `src/agents/tools/web-search-provider-config.ts` was already fixed by #86818.

Fixes #87191

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Gateway crash-loops on startup with "tools.web.search.<provider>: legacy web_search provider config must use plugins.entries.<plugin>.config.webSearch", even when no legacy config is present in user config, because the brave extension's inline merge re-enumerates runtime-injected legacy keys.
- **Real environment tested:** Worktree at /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-87191 with vitest
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  npx vitest run extensions/brave/src/brave-web-search-provider.test.ts --no-color
  ```
  The brave extension tests cover the mergeScopedSearchConfig code path.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  ✓ extensions/brave/src/brave-web-search-provider.test.ts (26 tests) 1.84s
    Test Files  1 passed (1)
    Tests  26 passed (26)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** All 26 brave tests pass. The inline mergeScopedSearchConfig now uses `Object.defineProperty` with `enumerable: false` for legacy provider keys that were runtime-injected, matching the shared module behavior from #86818.
- **What was not tested:** Did not start a live OpenClaw gateway with multiple plugins to reproduce the full crash-loop. The fix is a source-level code alignment change — making the brave inline copy match the already-proven shared implementation.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test (existing)
- Target test file: `extensions/brave/src/brave-web-search-provider.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: The brave mergeScopedSearchConfig now treats legacy provider keys the same as the shared module — runtime-injected keys without a prior own-property descriptor are made non-enumerable.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The existing 26 brave tests verify the function continues to work correctly. The change aligns the inline copy with the already-tested shared implementation.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The brave extension had an inline copy of `mergeScopedSearchConfig` that used simple object spread (`{...searchConfig, [key]: {...}}`) instead of `Object.defineProperty` with `enumerable: false` for legacy provider keys. This inline copy pre-dated PR #86818 which fixed the shared module but did not update the brave-specific copy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No CI/lint check ensures that provider-specific merge functions stay synchronized with the shared `mergeScopedSearchConfig`.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
